title: Specify lead reconciliation envelope
description: Validates the request and response envelopes of the agent-led lead-reconciliation step per RFC-29 D2. `kind: request` is returned by `specrun plan propose --dry-run --format json`: a flat catalog of raw, unmerged `(source-key, lead-id)` leads read 1:1 from `discovery.md`, plus the `projects[]` topology (always at least one project; each entry carries the normalized target adapter the kernel writes to `plan.yaml` at projection time). Optional `aliases[]` on each row are hints from `discovery.md`; the kernel does not lock or auto-merge on them. `kind: response` is the agent's grouping consumed by `specrun plan propose --from`: a single `slices[]` list, each row carrying a `scope` id, its matched `sources[]` (at most one lead per source), and a bound `project` from the request (the kernel auto-binds the sole project when only one exists and derives each slice's `target` from the bound project's `projects[].target`). Slices sharing a `scope` id form a fan-out group and MUST carry an identical `sources[]` set; that per-scope set partitions the surveyed leads exactly once and a scope never names two leads from the same source. The kernel validates this shape, enforces the global partition invariant, rejects same-source fusion, derives unique `plan.yaml.slices[]` names from the optional `name` or a `scope` / `scope`-plus-`project` fallback, and writes the structured `{ source-key, lead-id }` source bindings through the existing plan writers only when the plan is still replaceable; cross-source matching is agent judgment surfaced for Gate 1 review — the kernel never auto-merges leads. Closed top-level shape: unknown fields are rejected.

targetRef.description: Target adapter identifier, e.g. `omnia@v1` or `contracts@v1`. Mirrors `plan.schema.json` slice `target` pattern.

projectRef.description: One project the agent can bind a response slice to. For a hub this is a `registry.yaml#/projects[]` entry; for a single regular project the CLI synthesizes one entry from `project.yaml` (name + target adapter + domain). The agent matches the slice's scope against the project's `target` and `description`.

name.description: Registry project name; the value the kernel writes to `plan.yaml.slices[].project`.

target.description: The project's registered target adapter. The kernel writes this value to `plan.yaml.slices[].target` when the agent binds the slice to this project.

description.description: Single-sentence domain characterisation used by the agent when more than one project shares a target.

lead-id.description: Discovery lead id surfaced by this source binding. Identity is the `(source-key, lead-id)` pair; `lead-id` is not unique across `leads[]` when multiple sources surface the same slug.

summary.description: One-line per-source summary from this source's survey of the lead. Primary signal for agent cross-source grouping.

aliases.description: Optional alias hints from `discovery.md` (survey-time or operator-authored via `specrun plan amend --add-alias`). The agent may use these when grouping; the kernel does not lock on them.

projects.description: Project topology the agent binds slices to — always at least one project, so there is no separate single-repo shape. For a hub this is every entry from `registry.yaml#/projects[]`; for a single regular project the CLI synthesizes one entry from `project.yaml` (name + resolved target adapter + domain). Persisted project configuration is normalized into this envelope-local shape.

leads.description: Flat lead catalog: one row per raw `(source-key, lead-id)` lead, read 1:1 from `discovery.md` (no expansion or cross-source merge). The `lead-id` field repeats across rows when multiple sources surface the same slug.

source-key.description: Plan source binding key; must match a request catalog row.

lead-id.description: Discovery lead id; with `source-key`, must match a request catalog row.

name.description: Optional explicit plan slice name for this `(scope, project)` row — the slice's identity and the natural top field of a response slice. When omitted, the kernel derives a unique slice name deterministically from `scope` (or `scope`-plus-`project` on fan-out).

scope.description: Logical id of the reconciled unit of work this slice belongs to. It is the slice-name basis and the fan-out grouping key, not necessarily the derived plan slice name. A `scope` id repeats across response slices only when the same scope fans out to multiple projects; every slice sharing it MUST carry an identical `sources[]` set (the kernel enforces this as `plan-reconcile-fanout-source-mismatch`).

sources.description: Matched catalog rows for this slice, each referenced by `{ source-key, lead-id }`. At most one entry per `source-key`: a scope matches leads across sources but never fuses two leads from the same source (the kernel enforces this as `plan-reconcile-slice-source-collision`). Slices sharing a `scope` id repeat this set verbatim. The kernel writes it to `plan.yaml.slices[].sources[]`.

rationale.description: Agent's reason for the cross-source match in this scope. Scope-level — attach on any one slice in a fan-out group; the kernel dedupes by `scope` for the `plan.reconcile.agent` journal payload and `change.md`. The agent renders it into `change.md` for Gate 1 review. Recommended when the match is not obvious from shared slugs or alias hints.

depends-on.description: Derived slice names this row depends on (not scope ids). Each name must resolve to a projected `plan.yaml.slices[]` entry.

project.description: Project this slice binds to, chosen by the agent from the request's `projects[]`. Required on every slice when the request offers more than one project; optional when exactly one project exists, in which case the kernel auto-binds it and derives `target` from that project's entry.
